floating_rate_obligations: CenterPoint Energy: $1.5 billion (primarily commercial paper and short-term borrowings); Houston Electric: $500 million (maturing Q1 2026); CERC: $559 million (commercial paper). 100bp increase would result in: CenterPoint $15 million, Houston Electric $5 million, CERC $6 million annual interest expense increases
fixed_rate_debt: CenterPoint Energy: $21.7 billion principal, $21.1 billion fair value; Houston Electric: $9.7 billion principal, $8.9 billion fair value; CERC: $4.2 billion principal, $4.2 billion fair value
fair_value_sensitivity: CenterPoint Energy: $800 million increase; Houston Electric: $400 million increase; CERC: $120 million increase; ZENS debt component: less than $1 million increase
zens_derivative_component: $564 million recorded liability at December 31, 2025. If risk-free interest rate increases by 10%, fair value would decrease by less than $1 million
equity_holdings: 10.2 million shares of AT&T Common; 0.9 million shares of Charter Common; 2.5 million shares of WBD Common
commodity_risk_exposure: Exposed to commodity price risk during severe weather events (hurricanes, tornadoes, severe winter weather) affecting natural gas, coal and purchased power prices. Indiana regulated operations have limited exposure due to state regulations allowing cost recovery through adjustment mechanisms. Authority to lock in pricing for up to 50% of annual natural gas purchases using arrangements with original term up to 10 years
